question: Will the price of XRP be above $2.00 on March 24?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for XRP/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Saint Helena - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Eswatini and Saint Helena scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Saint Helena
events.description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Eswatini and Saint Helena scheduled for March 24 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Eswatini enters as heavy favorites in trader consensus for their T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash against Saint Helena, driven by superior squad depth and recent tournament form. Eswatini notched a convincing 104-run win over Seychelles earlier this week, showcasing balanced bowling led by Ringane Magagula's 3 wickets, while Saint Helena suffered a 168-run drubbing by Rwanda, exposing batting frailties averaging under 50. No head-to-head history exists, but Eswatini's higher ICC rankings and experience in regional qualifiers tilt probabilities sharply. Neutral venue in Uganda favors the more cohesive side; watch for dew impacting second innings chasing, though upsets remain possible in unpredictable associate cricket.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Saint Helena - Toss Match Double Eswatini Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Eswatini and Saint Helena scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Eswatini will be considered correct if  Eswatini is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if Saint Helena is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Saint Helena - Most Sixes Eswatini Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Eswatini and Saint Helena scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Eswatini will be considered correct if Eswatini is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Saint Helena.The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if Saint Helena is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Eswatini.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash between Eswatini and Saint Helena, trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest for most sixes, with all outcomes hovering near even money due to both sides' anemic power-hitting. Eswatini's batsmen managed just 4 sixes across their last three outings in bowler-friendly conditions at Dar es Salaam, while Saint Helena cleared the ropes only twice in recent qualifiers, underscoring mutual struggles with boundary clearance. No major lineup shifts or injuries reported, and historical head-to-heads (none recent) amplify uncertainty; low-scoring trends favor a draw, as aggressive T20 strokeplay remains elusive for these minnows, keeping markets tightly bunched.

Low-scoring venues like Entebbe Oval favor bowlers, compressing top run tallies historically under 40, while head-to-head inexperience and similar group stage averages (Eswatini 12.5 runs/batter, SAI 13.2) keep dynamics balanced amid rest parity and no major injuries reported.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Saint Helena - Team Top Batter Draw
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Eswatini and Saint Helena scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Eswatini will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Eswatini.
The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Saint Helena.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Ghana vs Seychelles
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Ghana and Seychelles scheduled for March 24 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ghana enters as clear trader favorite against Seychelles in this T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash, driven by superior recent form and squad experience in Dar es Salaam. Ghana secured two wins earlier in the tournament, led by opener Obed Asare's explosive batting (87 runs vs Gambia) and spinner Vincent Vanetsi's wickets, building momentum toward semi-final hopes. Seychelles faltered with heavy losses to Gambia and St Helena, exposed by weak bowling averages over 10 runs per over. No head-to-head history exists, but Ghana's 87th ICC ranking dwarfs Seychelles' unranked status. Pitch conditions favor Ghana's pace attack, with no injury disruptions reported; rest advantage tilts odds further amid qualifier volatility.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Ghana vs Seychelles - Toss Match Double Ghana Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Ghana and Seychelles scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if  Ghana is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if Seychelles is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Ghana vs Seychelles - Most Sixes Ghana Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Ghana and Seychelles scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if Ghana is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Seychelles.The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if Seychelles is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Ghana.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Legends Cricket League: Mumbai Spartans vs Royal Riders Punjab
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Mumbai Spartans and Royal Riders Punjab scheduled for March 23 2026 in Legends Cricket League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

description:

If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.

eventMetadata.context_description: India Maharajas hold a slim edge over Asia Lions atop the Legends League Cricket standings, both with three wins from four matches and six points apiece, separated by net run rate; this clash could lock in the top spot for direct finals qualification. Maharajas' recent momentum stems from Suresh Raina's explosive batting and Pravin Tambe's wickets, while Lions rely on Shahid Afridi's all-round threat and Misbah-ul-Haq's finishing. Head-to-head favors Maharajas from prior LLC editions, but Lions' spin duo of Imran Tahir and Abdur Razzak thrives on the turning pitches at Lauderhill. Trader consensus reflects a close contest, with rest advantages minimal ahead of playoffs—watch for toss and early breakthroughs shaping probabilities.

question: Legends Cricket League: 1st Place vs 2nd Place - Most Sixes 1st Place Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between 1st Place and 2nd Place scheduled for 2026-03-24 in Legends Cricket League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to 1st Place will be considered correct if 1st Place is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than 2nd Place.The outcome corresponding to 2nd Place will be considered correct if 2nd Place is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than 1st Place.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: India Capitals, holding first place in the Legends League Cricket standings, edge Gujarat Giants (second place) by just one six in the season-long most sixes race—52 to 51—fueling the near-even trader consensus at 50.5% for 1st, 50% draw, and 50% for 2nd. Recent qualifier clashes saw Capitals' Martin Guptill and Gurkeerat Mann unleash boundaries, narrowing the gap after Giants dominated mid-season with Thisara Perera's power-hitting. With finals looming on October 6, both squads boast explosive T20 veterans and favorable batting conditions in Jaipur, keeping outcomes unpredictable amid rest advantages and pitch familiarity that could swing momentum either way.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the NFL trade deadline looming at 4 p.m. ET on October 29, trader consensus heavily favors Philadelphia Eagles edge rusher Haason Reddick as the most likely to move, driven by his ongoing holdout, trade request, and reports of active shopping by GM Howie Roseman amid a 2-5 start. Recent confirmed deals like Davante Adams to the Jets and lesser swaps (e.g., Browns' Za'Darius Smith to Ravens) signal buyer demand for pass rushers and depth, boosting odds for New Orleans Saints CB Marshon Lattimore and Panthers EDGE Brian Burns alternatives. Unconfirmed buzz swirls around 49ers WR Deebo Samuel, but official denials temper expectations; last-minute motivations like playoff pushes could spark upsets in this fluid market.

question: Spread: FC Fredericia (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Denmark Superliga game, scheduled for April 13 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Fredericia" if FC Fredericia win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Vejle BK".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on superligaen.dk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Fredericia vs. Vejle BK - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Denmark Superliga game, scheduled for April 13 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vejle BK's edge as a Superliga side fuels trader consensus favoring them over 1st Division hosts FC Fredericia in this Danish Cup clash, with implied probabilities reflecting Vejle's 65% win chance amid their solid away form (unbeaten in last four road games). Fredericia's resilient home record—three wins in five—adds upset potential, bolstered by no major injuries reported on either official roster. Recent developments include Vejle's midweek Superliga draw providing rest, while Fredericia rides momentum from a 2-1 league upset. Head-to-head favors Vejle (3-1 aggregate last two meetings), but cup dynamics and Fredericia's pressing style could tighten odds as kickoff nears.

question: Spread: Vejle BK (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Denmark Superliga game, scheduled for April 13 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Vejle BK" if Vejle BK win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Fredericia".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on superligaen.dk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Everton FC vs. Chelsea FC: O/U 9.5 Total Corners
description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Everton FC and Chelsea FC, scheduled for March 21, 2026 at 1:30 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Over" if there are 10 or more total corners awarded in this game (combining corners for both teams). If the total number of corners is fewer than 10, this market will resolve to "Under". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. This market refers only to corners awarded within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Corners awarded during extra time or penalty shootouts do not count. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Everton FC vs. Chelsea FC - Total Corners
events.description: Total corners markets for the Premier League game between Everton FC and Chelsea FC, scheduled for March 21, 2026 at 1:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chelsea's high-possession style under Enzo Maresca, averaging 6.1 corners per match in recent Premier League outings, drives trader consensus toward higher totals against Everton's compact defense, which has conceded 5.8 corners per game over their last five home fixtures. Everton generate fewer (4.2 average) but force opponents into wide areas via counters, boosting combined tallies—past head-to-heads averaged 10.3 corners. No major injuries to key fullbacks or wingers like James or Gusto for Chelsea, or Young for Everton, per official reports; rest advantages favor Chelsea post-midweek. Schedule pressure on Chelsea for top-four push could amplify attacking intent, though Everton's set-piece resilience tempers extremes.

events.description: Total corners markets for the Premier League game between Fulham FC and Burnley FC, scheduled for March 21, 2026 at 11:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fulham's aggressive home pressing and Burnley's compact defensive setup are the key drivers behind trader consensus on total corners, with recent matches averaging 11.2 combined for Fulham at Craven Cottage versus Burnley's road games yielding 9.8.
